title: Climate Solutions Pathway
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive simulation where students activate and combine climate solutions across five sectors to see projected temperature pathway outcomes

learningObjectives: Assess the relative impact of different climate solutions and explain how mitigation and adaptation strategies work together
Evaluate the feasibility and scalability of solutions across energy, transport, land use, industry, and policy sectors
Construct a portfolio of solutions that achieves meaningful emissions reductions
prerequisites: Understanding of the greenhouse effect and global warming
Familiarity with major sources of greenhouse gas emissions by sector

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Solutions-oriented portfolio building with comparative analysis
keyQuestions: Which sector offers the largest total emissions reduction potential?
Why is a portfolio approach more effective than relying on one solution?
What is the difference between mitigation and adaptation strategies?
What barriers prevent high-potential solutions from being widely adopted?
commonMisconceptions: Students often believe a single technology like solar power can solve climate change on its own, not recognizing the need for a portfolio of solutions across sectors
Many students confuse mitigation (reducing emissions) with adaptation (adjusting to climate impacts)
Students may think individual actions like recycling are sufficient without recognizing the need for systemic policy changes
assessmentOpportunities: Students build a climate solutions portfolio and justify their selections
Students compare sector-by-sector reduction potentials and explain the differences
Students research one real-world solution and connect simulation findings to implementation challenges
